active_question: If jute rope accidentally gets wet, how does Anatomie Studio suggest drying it to help restore its original form?
edited_answer: Anatomie Studio suggests drying the rope under tension.
edited_question: If jute rope accidentally gets wet, how does Anatomie Studio suggest drying it to help restore its original form?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement turns the source-supported imperative fragment into a complete attributed answer.
reason: The replacement turns the source-supported imperative fragment into a complete attributed answer.
support_evidence: The first thing you should know is that you generally don’t want to get your jute ropes wet. Anyone who’s had a glass or water spilled on their ropes will tell you that the ropes swell up, and the twist tightens making the ropes feel spongy and springy when they dry out. Note: if this does even happen to you, make sure you dry the ropes under tension to help them return to their original form. This is actually one of the factors that makes dyed ropes more expensive. While you could just chuck your set of jute ropes in the washing machine for an intensive wash and spin cycle we wouldn’t recommend it.

active_answer: Only when that individual has actually consented.
active_question: When does WykD’s newcomer article say a dominant has the right to tell a submissive what to do?
edited_answer: It says a dominant has that right only when the individual has actually consented.
edited_question: When does WykD’s newcomer article say a dominant has the right to tell a submissive what to do?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement turns the source-supported condition into a complete answer without weakening the consent requirement.
reason: The replacement turns the source-supported condition into a complete answer without weakening the consent requirement.
support_evidence: New dominants, you absolutely do not… have the right to tell any submissive whatsoever what to do. The only time you will is when you have actual consent from the individual. You have no automatic right to demand anything of anyone.
